prerequisites: An Okta organization (tenant) with admin access, plus your Okta domain (the subdomain from your org URL, for example MY_DOMAIN from https://MY_DOMAIN.okta.com).
Either a valid Okta API token, or an OAuth 2.0 application registered in your Okta org with the scopes needed for the resources you want to read (for example okta.users.read).
Optionally, a start_date to bound how far back historical records are fetched. If omitted, Okta source defaults to 7 days of history.
Network/firewall access to your Okta org over HTTPS; calls are subject to Okta API rate limits.

authMethods.name: API token (SSWS)
authMethods.summary: Uses the credentials.api_token secret. Create an API token in the Okta Admin Console (Security > API > Tokens). The token inherits the privilege level of the admin account that created it, so a dedicated service account is recommended. Requests authenticate by sending the token in the HTTP Authorization header with the SSWS scheme (Authorization: SSWS <token>). Record the token at creation time; it cannot be viewed again afterward.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Uses credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret with a credentials.refresh_token. Register an OAuth 2.0 (OIDC) app in the Okta Admin Console, have a Super Admin grant the required Okta API scopes (for example okta.users.read), and obtain a refresh token. Only the org authorization server can mint access tokens carrying Okta API scopes.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 with private key JWT
authMethods.summary: A service-app variant that uses credentials.client_id with credentials.private_key and credentials.key_id (kid) instead of a client secret. The app authenticates with Okta's private_key_jwt client authentication method by signing a JWT with the private key (RS256/ES256 family); the corresponding public key must be registered in the app's JWKSet. The signed JWT is sent as client_assertion with client_assertion_type urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer.

setupSteps.title: Find your Okta domain
setupSteps.body: Take the subdomain from your Okta org URL and use it as the domain config value. If your URL is https://MY_DOMAIN.okta.com/, the domain is MY_DOMAIN.

setupSteps.title: Choose and obtain credentials
setupSteps.body: For API token auth, create a token in the Okta Admin Console under Security > API > Tokens and copy it immediately (it is shown only once). For OAuth, register an OIDC application in the Admin Console, have a Super Admin grant the Okta API scopes you need (for example okta.users.read), and capture the client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token. For the private key JWT variant, register the public key in the app's JWKSet and keep the matching private_key and its key_id (kid).

setupSteps.title: Set the credentials in the connector
setupSteps.body: Populate the credentials object with your chosen method: api_token for SSWS auth, or client_id/client_secret/refresh_token (optionally key_id/private_key for private key JWT) for OAuth 2.0. The connector sends these to your Okta org to authenticate API calls.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to control how far back historical records are pulled. If you leave it empty, the Okta source defaults to the last 7 days.
